heading: Education loan repayment program for members on active duty in specified military specialties
label: 2772 Education loan repayment program for members on active duty in specified military specialties
notes: References in TextThe Higher Education Act of 1965, referred to in subsec. (a)(1)(A) to (C), is , , . Parts B, D, and E of title IV of the Act are classified generally to parts B (§ 1071 et seq.), D (§ 1087a et seq.), and E (§ 1087aa et seq.), respectively, of subchapter IV of chapter 28 of Title 20, Education. For complete classification of this Act to the Code, see Short Title note set out under and Tables.Pub. L. 89–32979 Stat. 1219section 1001 of Title 20Nov. 8, 1965

Amendments2022— amended section generally. Prior to amendment, section related to education loan repayment program.Pub. L. 117–2632021—Subsec. (a)(2). substituted “individual” for “person”.Pub. L. 116–2832018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–282section 472 of this title

children.label: A
children.text: any loan made, insured, or guaranteed under part B of title IV of the Higher Education Act of 1965 ( et seq.);20 U.S.C. 1071

children.label: B
children.text: any loan made under part D of such title (the William D. Ford Federal Direct Loan Program, et seq.);20 U.S.C. 1087a

children.label: C
children.text: any loan made under part E of such title ( et seq.); or20 U.S.C. 1087aa

children.label: i
children.text: an agency or instrumentality of a State;

children.label: ii
children.text: a financial or credit institution (including an insurance company) that is subject to examination and supervision by an agency of the United States or any State;

children.label: iii
children.text: a pension fund approved by the Secretary for purposes of this section; or

children.label: iv
children.text: a nonprofit private entity designated by a State, regulated by such State, and approved by the Secretary for purposes of this section.

children.heading: .—Requirement
children.label: 2 .—Requirement
children.text: Repayment of any such loan shall be made on the basis of each complete year of service performed by the borrower.

children.heading: .—Eligibility
children.label: 3 .—Eligibility
children.text: The Secretary may repay loans described in paragraph (1) in the case of any person for service performed on active duty as a member in an officer program or military specialty specified by the Secretary.

children.heading: .—Amount
children.label: b .—Amount
children.text: The portion or amount of a loan that may be repaid under subsection (a) is 33⅓ percent or $1,500, whichever is greater, for each year of service.

children.heading: .—Interest Accrual
children.label: c .—Interest Accrual
children.text: If a portion of a loan is repaid under this section for any year, interest on the remainder of such loan shall accrue and be paid in the same manner as is otherwise required.

children.heading: .—Rule of Construction
children.label: d .—Rule of Construction
children.text: Nothing in this section shall be construed to authorize refunding any repayment of a loan.

children.heading: .—Fractional Credit for Transfer
children.label: e .—Fractional Credit for Transfer
children.text: An individual who transfers from service making the individual eligible for repayment of loans under this section (as described in subsection (a)(3)) to service making the individual eligible for repayment of loans under (as described in subsection (a)(2) or (g) of that section) during a year shall be eligible to have repaid a portion of such loan determined by giving appropriate fractional credit for each portion of the year so served, in accordance with regulations of the Secretary concerned.section 16301 of title 10

children.heading: .—Schedule for Allocation
children.label: f .—Schedule for Allocation
children.text: The Secretary shall prescribe a schedule for the allocation of funds made available to carry out the provisions of this section and during any year for which funds are not sufficient to pay the sum of the amounts eligible for repayment under subsection (a) and .section 16301 of title 10section 16301(a) of title 10

children.heading: .—Failure to Complete Period of Service
children.label: g .—Failure to Complete Period of Service
children.text: Except an individual described in subsection (e) who transfers to service making the individual eligible for repayment of loans under , a member of the Coast Guard who fails to complete the period of service required to qualify for loan repayment under this section shall be subject to the repayment provisions of section 303a(e) or 373 of title 37.section 16301 of title 10

children.heading: .—Authority to Issue Regulations
children.label: h .—Authority to Issue Regulations
children.text: The Secretary may prescribe procedures for implementing this section, including standards for qualified loans and authorized payees and other terms and conditions for making loan repayments. Such regulations may include exceptions that would allow for the payment as a lump sum of any loan repayment due to a member under a written agreement that existed at the time of a member’s death or disability.
